item.title: Neurocrine to buy Soleno Therapeutics for $2.9 billion
item.description: Neurocrine Biosciences will acquire rare disease drugmaker ​Soleno Therapeutics for $2.9 billion in ‌cash, the companies said on Monday, marking the neuroscience-focused drugmaker's expansion into ​metabolic disorders.

item.title: Madison Air targets $13.2 billion valuation in US IPO
item.description: Ventilation and ‌filtration systems provider Madison Air Solutions said on ​Monday it was ​targeting a valuation of ⁠up to $13.2 billion ​in its initial public ​offering in the United States.

item.title: JPMorgan's Dimon warns Iran war may drive inflation and interest rates higher
item.description: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon warned on Monday that the war in Iran risks oil and commodity ​price shocks that could keep inflation sticky and push interest rates higher than the market now expects.

item.title: US crude premiums climb to record levels as Asia, Europe compete for supply
item.description: Spot premiums for U.S. West Texas Intermediate crude have jumped ​to all-time highs as competition between Asian and European refiners for supply heats up ‌to replace Middle Eastern oil flows disrupted by the Iran war, industry sources said.

item.title: U.S. auto safety regulator closes probe into Tesla's driver assistance feature
item.description: The National ​Highway ‌Traffic Safety ​Administration ​said on Monday ⁠that ​it ​had closed a ​probe ​into Tesla vehicles' "Actually ‌Smart ⁠Summon" feature, covering ​roughly ​2.59 ⁠million ​vehicles.

item.title: US stock futures edge up as investors assess Mideast ceasefire prospects
item.description: U.S. stock index futures inched ​higher on Monday, after the main indexes marked their biggest weekly jump in four ‌months in the last session, as investors assessed prospects of an end to the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Global equity funds draw second weekly inflow amid war de-escalation hopes
item.description: Investors bought global equity funds worth a net $15.02 billion in the March 26-April 1 period, logging a second ​straight week of inflows, on hopes that the U.S.-Israeli war ‌with Iran could soon de-escalate.

item.title: Indian IT firms face subdued fourth quarter as war, AI concerns persist; weak rupee helps earnings
item.description: Top Indian information technology firms are set to report another lacklustre quarter, with revenue and profit seen rising around 10% year-on-year largely on a weaker rupee rather than underlying growth, seven ​brokerages said.

item.title: Tesla's South Korean sales up more than 300% to 11,134 vehicles in March
item.description: Tesla saw ​its car registrations ‌in South Korea rise ​330% ​to 11,134 vehicles in ⁠March ​from year earlier, ​market researcher Carisyou said on ​Monday.

item.title: Citigroup pushes back Fed rate cut timeline after strong job numbers
item.description: Citigroup has pushed back its Fed rate-cut timeline, citing unexpectedly strong U.S. ​job gains and persistent inflation risks.

item.title: India's Wipro rises after deal to acquire Olam Group’s IT business
item.description: Shares of Wipro rose as much as 3.2% on Monday after the Indian IT ​services firm agreed to buy the IT services business ‌of Singapore-based Olam Group for an enterprise value of $375 million.

item.title: UPS, Teamsters reach settlement that caps driver severance offers
item.description: United Parcel Service said on Sunday that it had ​reached an agreement with the ‌International Brotherhood of Teamsters to cap severance offers at 7,500 drivers after ​a dispute over the ​company's plans to cut its workforce.

item.title: Asia markets brace for Trump's promised assault on Iranian infrastructure
item.description: Oil prices rose, bonds fell and stocks were ​mixed at the start of trading in Asia on Monday.

item.title: Dollar steady as traders fret about escalating Iran war
item.description: The dollar was steady, while the yen flirted with the crucial 160 per dollar level.

item.title: How one factory in China learned to live with Trump, tariffs and turmoil
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump's tariffs sought to hurt Chinese manufacturing, but for one electronics maker, a turbulent 2025 ended with a belief that China is a location that is difficult to replicate - as long as things don't change too drastically.

item.title: Gulf funds near deal to back Paramount's $81 billion takeover of Warner, WSJ reports
item.description: Paramount is ​in talks to secure ⁠signed equity ​commitments of ​close to $24 billion from three sovereign-wealth ​funds led ​by Saudi Arabia to ‌help ⁠back its takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery , ​the ​Wall ⁠Street Journal reported on ​Sunday.

item.title: Oil prices open higher as US-Israeli war with Iran continues to disrupt supply
item.description: Oil prices opened higher on ​Monday after the Easter ‌holiday weekend as the U.S.-Israeli war with ​Iran continues to ​disrupt global oil supply.

item.title: OPEC+ debates theoretical oil output hike amid Iran war paralysis, sources say
item.description: OPEC+ may approve an oil output increase on Sunday, four sources from the group said, a rise that will largely exist on paper as its key members are unable to raise production due ​to the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran.

item.title: Foxconn first-quarter revenue jumps, company cautions on geopolitics
item.description: Taiwan's ​Foxconn, the world's largest contract electronics maker, reported a 29.7% ‌on-year rise in first-quarter revenue on strong demand for artificial intelligence products, though it cautioned about "volatile" global politics.

item.title: War weighs on Egypt's private sector as PMI hits near two-year low in March
item.description: Egypt's non-oil private sector deteriorated at its sharpest pace in almost two years in March, as the Middle East wardrove ​up costs and dampened client demand, a closely watched ‌business survey showed on Sunday.

item.title: Britain woos expansion effort by Anthropic after US defence clash, FT says
item.description: Britain is trying ​to tempt Anthropic ‌to expand its presence ​in ​the country, as it ⁠seeks ​to capitalise on ​a fight between the maker ​of artifical ​intelligence app Claude ‌and ⁠the U.S. defence department, the Financial ​Times ​said ⁠on Sunday.

item.title: Striking JBS workers to return to Colorado plant on promise of talks
item.description: Workers of the world's largest meat company, JBS , agreed to return to work at a beef plant in Greely, Colorado after ​it agreed to resume negotiations, bringing a three-week picket to ‌an end, their union said in a statement on Saturday.

item.title: South Korea asks Gulf nations for steady energy supply, safety of Korean vessels
item.description: South Korean Finance Minister Koo Yun-cheol met with envoys from Gulf countries to ​shore up energy security and the ‌safety of Korean vessels near the Strait of Hormuz, the ministry said on Sunday, as the escalating Iran war ​disrupts shipping.

item.title: Taiwan says it has assurances over LNG supplies from 'major' country
item.description: Taiwan has received supply assurances from the energy minister of a "major" liquefied natural gas-producing country, the island's economy minister said on ​Saturday, speaking about the Iran war's impact on Middle East energy ‌imports.

item.title: ISS recommends vote against BP board over climate reporting
item.description: The proxy advisory made its recommendation for revoking some climate-reporting resolutions and allowing it ​to hold online-only shareholder ​meetings.

item.title: India makes first Iranian oil buy in seven years with no payment problems
item.description: Indian refiners have purchased Iranian oil amid the Middle ​East conflict that has disrupted supplies through ‌the Strait of Hormuz, the oil ministry said on Saturday.

item.title: Exclusive: Five EU finance ministers call for tax on energy companies' windfall profits
item.description: Five European Union finance ministers are calling for a ‌tax on windfall profits of energy companies in reaction to rising fuel prices due to the Iran war, according to a letter from the ministers to the EU Commission seen by Reuters on Saturday.

item.title: IMF urges BOJ to keep raising rates even as Iran war poses new risks
item.description: The International Monetary Fund urged the ‌Bank of Japan to continue raising interest rates, even as the Middle East war posed "significant new risks" to the country's economic outlook.

item.title: Telegram's Durov says Russia triggered payment system problem by blocking VPNs
item.description: Telegram founder Pavel Durov said on ​Saturday Russia's attempt to ‌block Virtual Private Networks triggered a problem with a ​domestic payment system, adding ​that tens of millions of ⁠Russians were now ​resisting the digital controls.

item.title: Lucid misses first-quarter vehicle delivery estimates
item.description: Lucid Group missed expectations ​for first-quarter vehicle ‌deliveries on Thursday, fanning concerns ​about weakening ​demand for its ⁠luxury sedans ​amid a broader ​electric-vehicle market slowdown.
